title: One Port, Many Consequences
theme: Perimeter ingress over Headscale with deny-by-default ACLs and hot-reloaded Traefik routes
style_anchor: dry technical realism with gentle cinematic tension
accent: one exposed 443 port, everything else politely hidden

protagonist.description: A compact Proxmox virtual machine running Tailscale/Headscale, Traefik, and CrowdSec; tidy rack-unit energy with a terminal-first personality
protagonist.silhouette: small upright server box with a faint status-LED glow and a network-cable halo

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

dry technical realism with gentle cinematic tension

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: wide.

Location: campus network operations wall. Scene: A campus edge diagram is simplified with almost suspicious efficiency: dozens of prior firewall exceptions get crossed out until only one external rule remains, pointing at a single perimeter node. The internal firewall behind it is left looking relieved, if slightly underemployed.

Recurring protagonist (ingress_vm): A compact Proxmox virtual machine running Tailscale/Headscale, Traefik, and CrowdSec; tidy rack-unit energy with a terminal-first personality Silhouette: small upright server box with a faint status-LED glow and a network-cable halo

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: one exposed 443 port, everything else politely hidden.

Visible prop continuity:
- prop_campus_external_firewall: single 443 rule; A laminated firewall note showing exactly one inbound exception: TCP 443 to the perimeter ingress node.; stable external edge rule; reuse whenever the one-public-port idea returns
- prop_internal_firewall_blank: zero public rules; An internal firewall status board with no public-facing entries at all.; stable internal firewall absence; reuse when the department side stays clean

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
prop_campus_external_firewall: single 443 rule; A laminated firewall note showing exactly one inbound exception: TCP 443 to the perimeter ingress node.; continuity: stable external edge rule; reuse whenever the one-public-port idea returns
prop_internal_firewall_blank: zero public rules; An internal firewall status board with no public-facing entries at all.; continuity: stable internal firewall absence; reuse when the department side stays clean
prop_proxmox_vm_create_script: build script; A script named proxmox-vm-create.sh, ready to provision the perimeter node as a proper virtual machine.; continuity: stable provisioning artifact for the same node build path
prop_proxmox_host: Proxmox host; A clean Proxmox management screen showing the VM being created with predictable resource limits.; continuity: same host environment for the ingress VM
prop_headscale_acl_policy_hujson: ACL policy file; headscale-acl-policy.hujson with explicit tags and narrow allowances for ingress-to-backend and sysadmin-only admin access.; continuity: stable policy source for tailnet reachability decisions
prop_tag_ingress_badge: tag:ingress; A small tag token identifying the perimeter node as the only thing allowed to reach web backends.; continuity: same ingress identity for the protagonist throughout
prop_tailscale_client: tailscale client; A Tailscale client running on the ingress VM and joined to the Headscale control plane as tag:ingress.; continuity: stable networking agent on the protagonist VM
prop_tailnet_map: tailnet map; A dynamic graph showing only approved internal paths, with SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox absent from the ingress node's options.; continuity: same tailnet topology view whenever internal reachability is discussed
prop_traefik_yml: traefik.yml; Static Traefik config defining entrypoints, ACME automation, and the CrowdSec bouncer plugin.; continuity: stable static edge proxy config for the node
prop_crowdsec_bouncer_plugin: CrowdSec bouncer; A plugin badge attached to Traefik, scanning for discourteous behavior and escorting it out.; continuity: same security layer integrated into Traefik
prop_dynamic_yml: dynamic.yml; The live Traefik route definitions file where new internal services are added as router+service blocks.; continuity: stable hot-reloaded routing manifest for the same ingress node
prop_hot_reload_indicator: hot-reload light; A small indicator that flips from amber to green the moment Traefik re-reads the dynamic config.; continuity: same reload feedback light for route changes
prop_department_firewall_panel: department firewall panel; A locked-down internal firewall display with no public rules and only ordinary private traffic routes.; continuity: stable internal boundary with zero public exposure
prop_old_exception_sticker: legacy exception sticker; A peeled sticker from the old world of per-backend exceptions, left only as evidence that life used to be tedious.; continuity: use only in scenes recalling the replaced design
prop_ingress_vm: perimeter ingress node; The VM itself, running Tailscale, Traefik, and CrowdSec as the single public edge for internal services.; continuity: same protagonist object across all scenes
prop_443_patch_cable: 443 uplink; A marked patch cable representing the only public ingress path, neatly routed and impossible to ignore.; continuity: stable representation of the single open port
prop_audit_summary_panel: audit summary; A final status panel confirming the single 443 exposure, the absence of internal public rules, and the live route model.; continuity: stable verification endpoint for the completed design
prop_green_check_mint: green check mint; A tiny mint-green checkmark token placed beside the report as a reward for everything working exactly as intended.; continuity: one-time celebratory detail for the completed rollout

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- Old design: many exceptions, many places to forget them.
- New design: one port, one node, one problem statement.

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: A campus edge diagram is simplified with almost suspicious efficiency: dozens of prior firewall exceptions get crossed out until only one external rule remains, pointing at a single perimeter node. The internal firewall behind it is left looking relieved, if slightly underemployed.
dialogue: Old design: many exceptions, many places to forget them.
New design: one port, one node, one problem statement.

props.label: single 443 rule
props.description: A laminated firewall note showing exactly one inbound exception: TCP 443 to the perimeter ingress node.
props.continuity: stable external edge rule; reuse whenever the one-public-port idea returns
props.interaction_detail: The rule count flashes from 17 to 1, with no confetti because compliance forbids confetti.

props.label: zero public rules
props.description: An internal firewall status board with no public-facing entries at all.
props.continuity: stable internal firewall absence; reuse when the department side stays clean
props.interaction_detail: The table is empty except for a note that says 'delightfully boring.'

props.label: build script
props.description: A script named proxmox-vm-create.sh, ready to provision the perimeter node as a proper virtual machine.
props.continuity: stable provisioning artifact for the same node build path
props.interaction_detail: The script prints a reassuring sequence of tasks and then adds, 'done, probably.'

props.label: Proxmox host
props.description: A clean Proxmox management screen showing the VM being created with predictable resource limits.
props.continuity: same host environment for the ingress VM
props.interaction_detail: CPU and RAM fields sit in rigid alignment, as if they, too, require change control.

dialogue: Static config belongs in static files; opinions belong elsewhere.
Let’s Encrypt handles trust, CrowdSec handles rude surprises.

props.label: traefik.yml
props.description: Static Traefik config defining entrypoints, ACME automation, and the CrowdSec bouncer plugin.
props.continuity: stable static edge proxy config for the node
props.interaction_detail: A corner of the file is labeled 'entrypoints' like it expects a boarding pass.

props.label: CrowdSec bouncer
props.description: A plugin badge attached to Traefik, scanning for discourteous behavior and escorting it out.
props.continuity: same security layer integrated into Traefik
props.interaction_detail: It stamps a few suspicious requests and sends them away with bureaucratic efficiency.

Recurring protagonist (ingress_vm): A compact Proxmox virtual machine running Tailscale/Headscale, Traefik, and CrowdSec; tidy rack-unit energy with a terminal-first personality Silhouette: small upright server box with a faint status-LED glow and a network-cable halo

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
prop_campus_external_firewall: single 443 rule; A laminated firewall note showing exactly one inbound exception: TCP 443 to the perimeter ingress node.; continuity: stable external edge rule; reuse whenever the one-public-port idea returns

prop_proxmox_host: Proxmox host; A clean Proxmox management screen showing the VM being created with predictable resource limits.; continuity: same host environment for the ingress VM
prop_headscale_acl_policy_hujson: ACL policy file; headscale-acl-policy.hujson with explicit tags and narrow allowances for ingress-to-backend and sysadmin-only admin access.; continuity: stable policy source for tailnet reachability decisions
prop_tag_ingress_badge: tag:ingress; A small tag token identifying the perimeter node as the only thing allowed to reach web backends.; continuity: same ingress identity for the protagonist throughout
prop_tailscale_client: tailscale client; A Tailscale client running on the ingress VM and joined to the Headscale control plane as tag:ingress.; continuity: stable networking agent on the protagonist VM
prop_tailnet_map: tailnet map; A dynamic graph showing only approved internal paths, with SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox absent from the ingress node's options.; continuity: same tailnet topology view whenever internal reachability is discussed

prop_hot_reload_indicator: hot-reload light; A small indicator that flips from amber to green the moment Traefik re-reads the dynamic config.; continuity: same reload feedback light for route changes
prop_department_firewall_panel: department firewall panel; A locked-down internal firewall display with no public rules and only ordinary private traffic routes.; continuity: stable internal boundary with zero public exposure
prop_old_exception_sticker: legacy exception sticker; A peeled sticker from the old world of per-backend exceptions, left only as evidence that life used to be tedious.; continuity: use only in scenes recalling the replaced design
prop_ingress_vm: perimeter ingress node; The VM itself, running Tailscale, Traefik, and CrowdSec as the single public edge for internal services.; continuity: same protagonist object across all scenes
prop_443_patch_cable: 443 uplink; A marked patch cable representing the only public ingress path, neatly routed and impossible to ignore.; continuity: stable representation of the single open port
